description: This map illustrates a 100m x 100m Primary Sample Unit (PSU), outlined in orange, overlaid on satellite imagery depicting a mixed landscape with green vegetated areas, white features (possibly buildings or bare ground), and some darker patches. Within this PSU, 20 red diamond markers are randomly distributed, each representing a Secondary Sample Unit (SSU) corresponding to a 5m x 5m High Resolution Layer (HRL) pixel. This visual demonstrates the sampling design used for validation purposes, specifically in the context of Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) products such as the HRL Small Woody Features (SWF).
